title: Miniature antenna array with polar combining architecture
abstract: A microcomponent massive MIMO array is presented. The microcomponent massive array includes a general purpose processor and an integrated power amplifier and transmitter device including a software defined radio (SDR) and a plurality of polar power amplifiers (PAs) disposed on a single integrated circuit, wherein the integrated power amplifier and transmitter device is in communication with the general purpose processor. The microcomponent massive MIMO array further includes an antenna array in communication with the integrated power amplifier and transmitter device.
claims: 1. An antenna array comprising: a plurality of antenna elements positioned around a singular axis of a support structure and mounted to the support structure, the antenna elements including integrated polar Power Amplifiers (PAs) and polar transmitters with phase control, the polar PAs thereby forming a polar phased array enabling beamforming; and wherein the plurality of antenna elements are each in communication with and coupled to a baseband processor used to facilitate signal transmissions using beamforming for a Radio Technology including at least one of 4G or 5G from one or more of the plurality of antenna elements positioned around the singular axis of the support structure toward an intended receiver; wherein the baseband processor coordinates the signal transmissions from at least one of the plurality of antenna elements with other transmitters of the plurality of antenna elements for sending to a same receiver from different locations, reinforcing the effective signal.
